findings.evidence: grep for 'relabelEquiv' in ./code returns no matches; lines 60-120 show the two definitions and their three lemmas each are identical modulo transpose/rotate.
findings.fix: Introduce GridChain.relabelEquiv (e : GridState n ≃ GridState n) := Finsupp.domLCongr e, prove relabelEquiv_single / relabelEquiv_apply / relabelEquiv_symm once (the symm lemma generalizes to (relabelEquiv e).symm = relabelEquiv e.symm), then define transposeEquiv and rotateEquiv as one-line instances and derive their six @[simp] lemmas from the generic ones. This is exactly the refactor the description states was performed.
findings.issue: transposeEquiv (line 60) and rotateEquiv (line 93) are parallel definitions differing only by the involution (GridState.transpose vs GridState.rotate); their lemmas transposeEquiv_single/rotateEquiv_single (66/99), transposeEquiv_apply/rotateEquiv_apply (74/107), and transposeEquiv_symm/rotateEquiv_symm (84/117) are verbatim clones. The PR description says this duplication is collapsed via GridChain.relabelEquiv, but that declaration is absent.

summary: The described factoring through a single GridChain.relabelEquiv was not done — no such declaration exists (grep: no matches). The diff only adopts Function.Involutive.toPerm (a correct, verified Mathlib reuse), leaving transposeEquiv/rotateEquiv and their six lemmas as verbatim parallel APIs differing only by the involution.
